note: 02_Feature_Registry.md describes an idealised API (defineFeature, lifecycle hooks, ctx.read, enum/string types, cacheKey, useFeatureParam, tab-dock auto-placement) that almost none of which exists in current code. The drift is large enough that it reads as an aspirational design doc rather than an out-of-date reference. Worth flagging whether this subsystem should rewrite the doc to current code or treat the doc as the migration target.
resolution: Addressed by Edit 2 (survey shape now has Recommendation slot); planner decision deferred to Phase 2.

note: Self-inconsistency in code: getAll() jsdoc at FeatureSystem.ts:443-447 says it throws on dependency cycle, but the implementation at :587-592 logs and falls back. This is a code-level bug, not a doc bug. Should drift entries like this be tracked separately from doc drift?
resolution: Code bug, not protocol issue. Survey already captures in body; out-of-scope for loop protocol.

note: Subsystem partitions sometimes too narrow: e09 audited the StateLibrary primitive but its user-facing surface (<StateLibraryPanel>, <StateLibraryToast>, <ActiveSnapshotFeatures>) lives in adjacent files not claimed by any subsystem. Consider an orphan-sweep step OR letting surveys nominate adjacent files as claimable by their subsystem.
resolution: Edit 2: Open questions section now supports Orphan-sweep candidate: prefix. Formal orphan-sweep phase deferred to post-Phase-1 (when gap shape is visible).

note: blob-sha.mjs accepts only one path argument (scripts/blob-sha.mjs:14-18) but the iteration brief phrases it as "<path>" per file, leading agents into a first-batch trap where they expect to pass multiple paths. Either clarify the brief ("call once per file") or extend the script to accept multiple args and emit a path:sha map.
resolution: Edit 1: blob-sha.mjs extended to multi-arg. Brief updated to show new usage. Single-arg backward-compatible.

summary: Sub-B orchestrator brief tells agents to call record-coverage.mjs with --path/--blob-sha/etc flags, but the script reads a YAML entry from stdin and requires fields: path, blob_sha, bytes, lines, read_range, agent_run_id, agent_model, timestamp, confidence, summary_ref. Multiple agents have figured it out anyway but the brief is misleading — should switch to a stdin-YAML heredoc example. Confidence values: high|medium|low|partial (no "full").
resolution: Primary observation (flag-vs-stdin interface) was in the orchestrator's spawn brief, not LOOP_PROMPT.md — orchestrator already updated per tick prompt; LOOP_PROMPT.md has no record-coverage references (grep returns zero). Secondary observation (confidence values) addressed by Edit 1: replaced stale `catalog`/`targeted` with valid `partial`/`high` at lines 142 and 342, with inline note about valid set {high, medium, low, partial}.

note: When the orchestrator passes "context from sibling investigation q-XXX" into a new investigation brief, it can anchor the agent on a wrong conclusion. q-030 found extraUniforms wasn't used for CamType (correct in that scope) and the orchestrator generalised that to "NOT from a separate extraUniforms array" in the q-031 brief. That generalisation turned out to be wrong — extraUniforms is the dominant path for lighting/env CDF uniforms. The investigator had to re-verify rather than trust the handed-down context. Either drop sibling-context handoff entirely, or mark it explicitly as tentative/scoped-to-prior-question so the next agent doesn't treat it as established fact.
resolution: Orchestrator behaviour issue, not a protocol gap — LOOP_PROMPT does not document sibling-context handoff; orchestrator added it freelance. Codifying "tentative" caveats would legitimise the pattern. Resolution: orchestrator stops the pattern. If a prior followup is genuinely load-bearing, pass its file path via target_hint so investigator reads source verbatim.
